stories.title: Russia hits Ukraine with one of the largest ballistic barrages of the war
stories.summary: Russia fired dozens of ballistic missiles at Ukraine overnight into Sunday in one of the heaviest attacks since the start of its full-scale invasion more than four years ago. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said the attack involved more than 40 missiles of various types and 120 attack drones. Ballistic missiles are especially hard to shoot down because of their speed, and Ukraine has run low on the U.S.-made Patriot interceptors that are its best defense against them. After the attack, U

stories.title: Health officials identify source of US explosive diarrhoea outbreak
stories.summary: The US Food and Drug Administration says iceberg lettuce supplied by Taylor Farms and sold at Taco Bell and Walmart locations was the source of cyclosporiasis infections.

stories.title: Hungary’s president agrees to stand down after law change ends his term
stories.summary: Tamás Sulyok signed the constitutional amendment that cited ‘serious loss of confidence’ in him as leader Hungary’s president, Tamás Sulyok, has agreed to step down after signing a constitutional amendment passed by the ruling Tisza party of the prime minister, Péter Magyar. The amendment will end Sulyok’s term immediately, citing society’s “serious loss of confidence” in a leader elected in early 2024 by lawmakers from the former prime minister Viktor Orbán’s nationalist Fidesz party. Continue

stories.title: Hungary's president signs amendment ending his own term
stories.summary: An ally of former PM Viktor Orban has signed a constitutional amendment ending his own term as head of state. Tamas Sulyok criticized the law, while lawmakers insist he has impeded national progress by staying in office.

stories.title: Hungary's president agrees to stand down after parliament backs removal
stories.summary: Tamás Sulyok, seen as an Orbán loyalist, had until Saturday night to agree to end his term.

stories.title: Hende Csaba sem maradhat alkotmánybíró egy korábbi törvény miatt
stories.summary: A második és harmadik Orbán-kormány honvédelmi minisztere nem az alkotmánybírókra vonatkozó életkori korlát miatt kénytelen távozni tisztségéből.

stories.title: Több mint két órán keresztül nem volt elérhető a Facebook, böngészőből
stories.summary: A részleges leállás okára nem adott magyarázatot az oldalt üzemeltető Meta.

stories.title: Kocsiszínbe tartó villamosok ütköztek Budapesten, az egyik jármű vezetője megsérült
stories.summary: A rendőrség szabálysértési eljárást indított.

stories.title: Minden idők legfontosabb tekergéstörténete, az Odüsszeia
stories.summary: Homérosz műve megihlette Krasznahorkait, Tolkient és Christopher Nolant, de a Némó nyomábanra és a Trónok harcára is hatott. Utánajártunk, miért aktuális ma is a lassan háromezer éves eposz.

stories.title: A vb-döntő napján állt bele a FIFA-ba az Európa Tanács főtitkára, szerinte a pályán megjelent a politikai befolyás
stories.summary: Alain Berset a Balogun-balhé mellett utalt a franciákra tett rasszista megjegyzésekre is a FIFA-t bíráló közleményében.

stories.title: Szijjártó Péter felhívta Szeged polgármesterét, hogy BYD-ügyben ahol tud, segít
stories.summary: Egyetlen percet beszélt Botka Lászlóval, aki csak a sajtóból értesült a volt külügyminiszter megbízatásáról.

stories.title: Antonellit meglepte a virtuális biztonsági autó, de Leclerc-t előzve így is behúzta a Belga Nagydíjat
stories.summary: Leclerc csak megszorongatni tudta a Mercedes 19 éves pilótáját. Hadjar a mezőny végéről indulva, három kerékcserével lett hatodik.

stories.title: Egy bukás miatt véget ért a második helyen álló Vingegaard Tour de France-a
stories.summary: A dán kerékpáros egy terelőszigetnél bukott, sérülése miatt fel kellett adnia a francia kerékpáros körversenyt a 15. szakaszon.

stories.title: Magyar Péter szerint hatalmas megtiszteltetés lenne, ha Polgár Judit vállalná az államfői szerepet, hétfőn találkoznak
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök hétfőn megkérdezi a sakkozót, hogy az új Alkotmány elfogadásáig „kész-e új szerepben szolgálni a hazánkat”.

stories.title: Kirúgták a MÁV-tól Orbán kampánykörútjának egyik résztvevőjét, akire a kampányban figyelt fel a Telex
stories.summary: Cserháti Gyula a Telex korábbi cikkei szerint rendszeres résztvevője volt a kampányban Orbán Viktor fórumainak. A miniszterelnököt éltető és az ellentüntetőket távoltartó csoporthoz tartozott.

stories.title: Nem biztos, hogy érdemes vendégmunkásokkal megmenteni a mostani magyar iparszerkezetet
stories.summary: Fáj az iparnak a júniusi vendégmunkásstop, de miért kezeljük gazdaságpolitikai alapvetésként, hogy a magyar gazdaságnak pontosan olyannak kell maradnia, mint jelenleg?

stories.title: A magyar állam mintegy 100 milliárd forint K+F-támogatást adott a német Rheinmetallnak, kérdés, ez megtérülhet-e valaha
stories.summary: A magyar állam és a német Rheinmetall 2023 végén harckocsifejlesztésre szerződött. Kutatásainkból kiderül, hogy ez azt jelentette: a magyar fél több mint 100 milliárd forinttal segítette a céget.

stories.title: Románia királyi tengerpartja, Mamaia mindig is a kiváltságosoknak szólt, de szerencsére nem csak ők kapták meg
stories.summary: Idén pontosan 120 éves az egyik leghíresebb román tengerparti üdülőhely.

stories.title: Hungarian president Tamás Sulyok signs constitutional amendment ending his own mandate
stories.summary: President Tamás Sulyok signed the seventeenth amendment to the Fundamental Law on Saturday, he announced on Facebook. The sitting president’s mandate will end the day after it takes effect.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Thunderstorms strike across Europe amid record heatwave
stories.summary: Storms are typical during intense heat but this week’s have been extreme. Plus, deadly monsoon rains in Bangladesh Hailstones the size of golf balls have been seen in French villages as, on top of the exceptional European heatwave, thunderstorms have struck across parts of Europe. While thunderstorms are typical during and after a period of extreme heat, the storms across countries such as France, Germany and Poland have been particularly severe, bringing flooding, strong winds and heavy showers

stories.title: Hungary's national fireworks costs shrink but many still call it wasteful
stories.summary: Tradition or money down the drain? A state-funded jolly for all, or an outdated form of animal cruelty? As 20 August nears, the yearly row over whether Hungary needs fireworks and how big they should be reignites.

stories.title: Some people want to break up the EU. Why don’t they want to break up the US?
stories.summary: The long-running series in which readers answer other readers’ questions poses a federal question Read replies: Why is there no rugby culture in Germany? If it’s wrong, as some would say, for European countries to come together as a fully federal European Union, rather than individual states, why is it right for the US to be a federation rather than separate states? It always strikes me that the EU gets unfairly criticised for a system that was established over the Atlantic many years back. The

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: US launches new round of airstrikes to ‘swiftly punish’ Iran after American troops killed
stories.summary: Kuwait reports attack from Tehran; Rubio meets with Lebanese president in Washington Two US troops killed and one missing in Jordan after Iranian attacks Jordanian authorities ⁠have not issued any decision ⁠to ​evacuate the airport or ⁠seaport in the city of Aqaba, and have not detected ⁠any potential threats ​in ‌the past ‌hours, the state ‌news agency cited the government spokesperson as saying. This denial comes in response to the US embassy in Jordan earlier saying the airport and seaport in

stories.title: US and Iran trade strikes again as Washington avenges American deaths
stories.summary: Tehran struck U.S. allies across the Persian Gulf region on Sunday and American forces bombed Iran for an eighth straight night, with the interim peace deal dead and neither side backing down. Last month’s agreement was meant to pause the fighting while the two sides worked toward reopening the Strait of Hormuz, which carries a fifth of the world’s traded oil in peacetime. But the accord has collapsed and Iran said on Saturday that it was suspending its commitments , accusing Washing

stories.title: Mission in Iran war has pivoted toward ensuring oil flow, Trump official says
stories.summary: Energy secretary grapples with mounting criticism over length of US war as Democrat calls conflict ‘war of choice’ A Trump administration official on Sunday grappled with mounting criticism over the length of the US war with Iran after months of promised diplomatic breakthroughs, short-lived ceasefires, and the deaths of two more American troops in Jordan bringing the total number of American military members killed in the conflict to 16. Appearing on ABC’s This Week, US energy secretary Chris W

stories.title: Cuban dissident Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara goes into exile in US
stories.summary: He was arrested in 2021 during Cuba's largest anti-government protests in decades and spent five years ⁠in prison.

stories.title: Merz floats government reshuffle after top conservative’s resignation
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz suggested Sunday that he could reshuffle his government following the resignation of Jens Spahn, the parliamentary leader of the Christian Democratic Union. Spahn quit on Saturday over revelations that he and his husband had a child through a surrogate mother in the United States. Surrogacy is banned in Germany, and the CDU reaffirmed its opposition earlier this year, exposing Spahn, a former health minister and one of the party’s most prominent figures, to

stories.title: Russia pounds Kyiv for five hours in one of its biggest ballistic missile attacks
stories.summary: Attack on Ukraine’s capital and other cities leaves at least six people dead and dozens injured Russia has carried out one of its biggest ballistic missile attacks on Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, launching a five-hour assault that left at least six people dead and dozens injured, and caused fires and damage across the capital. Ukrainian officials said the capital had been hit with about 40 Iskander-M and hypersonic Zircon missiles. Residents heard an air raid siren sound at 1.30am. There was

stories.title: Government use of automated AI decision-making to be curbed under new Australian rules
stories.summary: New national plan accompanied by Labor push for digital duty of care legislation Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The use of AI in automated decision-making by government departments and agencies will be subject to tough rules under a new national plan, expected to extend to consumer protections, workplace safety and privacy. As the Albanese government grapples with the rapid growth in the use of artificial intelligence and a boom in datacentre construction, senior mi

stories.title: ‘He’s a war criminal’: Mamdani exploring whether he can arrest Netanyahu if he visits New York
stories.summary: NYC mayor says in Times interview that’s he’s in ‘active conversation’ with city’s law department about the matter New York City mayor Zohran Mamdani has said he was reviewing whether his administration could arrest Benjamin Netanyahu if as expected the Israeli prime minister visits the city for the UN general assembly in September. Mamdani’s comments on Saturday’s episode of the New York Times’s the Interview podcast echoed ones he had made to the publication as he successfully ran for mayor la

stories.title: Thames Water creditors seek talks with Burnham as nationalisation looms
stories.summary: Consortium preparing for potential legal battle amid reports new PM could put the company into temporary public ownership The group of investors pursuing a rescue bid for Thames Water have said they are willing to discuss greater public control but are also preparing for a potential multi-billion pound legal battle amid reports that Andy Burnham could temporarily nationalise the company. London & Valley Water (L&VW), a consortium of 100 institutional investors that hold £17bn of the comp

stories.title: Two US troops killed and one missing in Jordan after Iranian attacks
stories.summary: Sirens sound in Bahrain and Kuwait accuses Tehran of targeting civilian sites and infrastructure as Iran strikes US allies The US retaliated against Iran’s Revolutionary Guard (IRGC) after two American troops were killed and one was missing in Jordan when Tehran launched a wave of attacks against US allies in the Middle East. Iran’s attacks came as the renewal of US strikes on Iran entered a second week and fighting escalated over the strait of Hormuz. Continue reading...

stories.title: Russian army deserters in Germany: 'No choice but to run'
stories.summary: Almost all asylum applications by Russian deserters are being denied by German authorities. DW and Russian-language media outlet Astra spoke to one of the young men who fears he'll be sent back to die on the front lines.

stories.title: Algeria seeks larger EU energy role amid regional tensions
stories.summary: Algeria and Germany agreed to deepen energy ties during President Abdelmadjid Tebboune's recent visit to Berlin. But Algiers is still grappling with tensions at home, over Western Sahara and instability in the Sahel.

stories.title: US and Iran trade fire after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
stories.summary: Kuwait says an electricity and water plant is on fire after the second such attack in two days. Iran says US strikes hit an under-construction nuclear power plant.

stories.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
stories.summary: Eight people die and dozens are injured as several cities are hit by missile and drone attacks, while Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.

stories.title: Trump administration activates never-before-used ‘alien terrorist’ deportation court
Ships continue to move through Strait of Hormuz, US Energy chief says
stories.summary: U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright said Sunday that despite renewed fighting with Iran, oil tankers continue to make it through the Strait of Hormuz. Speaking to host Jonathan Karl on ABC’s “This Week,” Wright said: “We’re at a little under 14 million barrels a day from the Arabian Gulf region. That’s two-thirds of pre-conflict traffic, dramatically up from where we were back in March when I was last on your show. I mean, not 100% yet, but two-thirds of flow right now.” Wrigh

stories.title: Victoria announces new social media ‘demasking’ powers for accounts accused of vilification
stories.summary: New laws would give Vcat power to force social and AI platforms to identify anonymous users in move premier says will protect children Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Social media companies could be forced to identify anonymous accounts accused of online vilification, under new laws being proposed in Victoria. The Victorian premier, Jacinta Allan, announced a suite of social media reforms on Sunday, saying families needed new ways to protect their children online. Co

stories.title: What happened to Nolan Wells? Family seeks answers two weeks after 18-year-old’s death
stories.summary: Investigation continues after college freshman was found dead on Mississippi island after 4 July boat trip with friends Two weeks after Nolan Xavier Wells was found dead, his family is still searching for answers. On 4 July, Wells, an 18-year-old Mississippi college freshman, and a group of friends took a boat out to Horn Island, a barrier island a few miles off the Mississippi Gulf coast. Continue reading...

stories.title: Australian and NZ safety assurances about asbestos in play sand were based on supplier’s own assessment
stories.summary: ACCC updates some recall notices to say products ‘may cause risk to health’, contrary to previous claims Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Schools, retailers and government agencies assured parents that asbestos-contaminated play sand posed a low risk to their children’s health based on a supplier’s own assessment which was not independently verified, the Guardian can reveal. The now-contested public messaging spread quickly after a range of Educational Colours childre

stories.title: Australia’s life dissatisfaction levels double as ‘wellbeing poverty’ reaches new highs
stories.summary: ABS data show one in 10 people are not satisfied with life – but community cohesion and trust lift happiness scores Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Nearly 2.2 million Australians 15 years and older are living below the wellbeing poverty line, with the share of people reporting very low life satisfaction doubling over the past decade. In 2025, 9.7%, or about one in 10 Australians, rated their general satisfaction with life at four or below out of 10, according to Guar

stories.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in US on UK rape and sex trafficking charges
stories.summary: Britain seeks extradition over charges including rape, trafficking for sexual exploitation and offences relating to extreme pornography The controversial influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested in the US as part of a UK police investigation into a number of alleged sexual offences. The Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) said it was bringing further charges in relation to four further individuals, and had requested the two men’s extradition. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘What has the BBC ever done for me?’ Romesh Ranganathan reboots corporation’s 1985 licence fee plea
stories.summary: As with the John Cleese original, comedian is supported by famous faces from Claudia Winkleman to David Jason John Cleese’s 1985 advert for the BBC in which he asks a pub full of stars “ What has the BBC ever given us? ” is one of the most famous examples of the corporation making the case to the public for the value of paying the licence fee. In the advert , Cleese is complaining to the bartender that he has just bought his TV licence. “Fifty-eight quid for the privilege of sitting in your own

stories.title: US boxer Hannah Rapp struck and killed by driver while cycling, police say
stories.summary: Investigators arrest 31-year-old man identified as Charles Medina in connection with death of Rapp who was 26 US boxer Hannah Rapp, who in June fought for the World Boxing Council (WBC) women’s featherweight championship, was killed on Saturday while she bicycled in Texas after a motorist struck her with his car, according to authorities. Rapp was 26, and investigators arrested a 31-year-old man identified as Charles Medina in connection with her death, the sheriff’s office of Brazos county, Tex

stories.title: Second person dies in legionnaires’ disease outbreak in New York City
stories.summary: Outbreak has sickened dozens in the city but officials say they believe source of outbreak ‘has likely been eliminated’ A second person has died amid a legionnaires’ disease outbreak that has sickened dozens of people in New York City , according to officials, who also say there is evidence that the source of the outbreak “has likely been eliminated”. New York City’s health department served notice of the second death in a statement on Saturday. The statement didn’t have any information about th

stories.title: Earthquake in Peru leaves at least five dead
stories.summary: More than 20 injured and 300 displaced after buildings collapse near Sicaya A 5.5-magnitude earthquake shook the Andes region of Peru, killing at least five people, authorities said Sunday. More than 20 people were injured and 300 have been displaced. The US Geological Survey reported the quake struck on Saturday at 9.24pm, with its epicentre located 2km (1.2 miles) west-south-west of the city of Sicaya in Huancayo province. It was 10km (6.2 miles) deep. Continue reading...

stories.title: ICE officers to use body cameras during vehicle stops, Trump ‘border czar’ says
stories.summary: Tom Homan’s comments come amid scrutiny over federal immigration agents’ fatal shooting of two men in six days US Immigration and Customs Enforcement ( ICE ) will require its officers to record vehicle stops with at least one body camera, said Tom Homan, the Trump administration “border czar”, on Sunday. The action comes as the agency is under renewed scrutiny after ICE officers fatally shot two men earlier this month. “They exonerate more law enforcement than they convict, and I want officers t

stories.title: Burnham’s vow to end Labour briefing wars will test his grip on the party
stories.summary: Tensions between his aims are already there, including disputes over key appointments such as chancellor Andy Burnham’s vision for cleaning up Westminster politics is a utopian one: ending the dominance of the whipping system, Whitehall power hoarding and the “insidious briefing culture” of the bubble. In his first speech on becoming Labour leader, he warned his party it “won’t beat Britain’s new right if we are consumed by infighting and pulling in different directions”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Burnham to say UK must be honest about its challenges as he prepares to become PM
stories.summary: Labour leader will visit king to accept role on Monday and will start making cabinet appointments that afternoon Andy Burnham will say Britain must be honest about the challenges it faces when he becomes its sixth prime minister in a decade on Monday, as he prepares to introduce measures to ease the cost of living and allow more North Sea fossil fuel drilling. The Labour leader is expected to start making appointments in the afternoon after going to Buckingham Palace to accept the role of prime

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Strikes devastate Russian warehouses ‘used for drones’
stories.summary: Ukraine hits Wildberries logistics centres in Moscow and Tambov regions; video shows moment of Monaco contract killing attempt. What we know on day 1,607 Continue reading...

stories.title: China firm seeks damages over state control of British Steel
stories.summary: The UK government took over British Steel last year to prevent the closure of the country's last primary steelmaking facility. China's Jingye Group, which bought the firm in 2020, claims it is owed compensation.

stories.title: EU ban on destroying unsold clothes takes effect
stories.summary: Under new rules to reduce waste, large companies across the European Union can no longer dispose of unsold clothes, accessories and footwear.

stories.title: Fact check: Spotting AI-generated 'historical' images
stories.summary: DW Fact check selected three "historical" videos that aren't real — they're AI. Could you recognize the fake? Here's how to spot it — and why it matters.

stories.title: Spain: Emergency crews battle wildfire north of Madrid
stories.summary: More than 25 firefighting planes have been deployed to battle the blaze in central Spain, which has been raging for three days.

stories.title: Germany news: Merz moots Cabinet reshuffle after Spahn exit
stories.summary: Chancellor Merz has said he might use the forced resignation of a senior ally as a chance to reshuffle his Cabinet. Meanwhile, a train security guard was critically injured in a clash with a passenger. DW has more.

stories.title: Israel clears way for crocodiles to stop prison breaks
stories.summary: Israel's environment minister has reclassified crocodiles so that they can now be used for security purposes. Far-right Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir reportedly wants them to surround a jail holding Hamas militants.

stories.title: England beat France 6-4 to claim World Cup third place
stories.summary: Bukayo Saka scored a hat trick as England beat France 6-4 in a record-breaking World Cup third-place playoff. The Three Lions survived a dramatic French comeback to secure their best finish at the tournament since winning the title in 1966.

stories.title: Neighbours actor Terence Donovan dies aged 90
stories.summary: His son, actor Jason Donovan, posted to social media that his father died on Saturday night peacefully in Melbourne, Australia

stories.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami as UK seeks extradition
stories.summary: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested in Miami ahead of an extradition request from the United Kingdom. British authorities accuse them of serious offences, including rape, human trafficking and sexual exploitation.

stories.title: Messi on the brink of history - will it be his last World Cup game?
stories.summary: Argentina captain Lionel Messi will play his 34th World Cup match in Sunday's final, but will it be his last appearance?

stories.title: Passenger ferry carrying 116 people sinks off the coast of Guyana
stories.summary: A distress call by the MV Barima to the coast guard was made just after 11pm local time. The vessel is said to be equipped with 250 life jackets, two rigid life boats and six inflatable life crafts.

stories.title: Magnitude 5.1 earthquake in central Peru leaves at least five dead and ten injured
stories.summary: A 5.1‑magnitude earthquake centred in Chupaca, Junín, left five people dead and around ten injured on Saturday night, and damaged hospitals and religious monuments in the Andean area.

stories.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
stories.summary: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.

stories.title: Runners complete 'world's coolest marathon' on drifting North Pole ice
stories.summary: Organisers say 23 runners from seven countries took part in races at the Arctic's geographic North Pole.

stories.title: The 1975's Matty Healy marries model Gabbriette Bechtel in Los Angeles
stories.summary: The newlyweds were first linked in 2023 after Healy's brief relationship with Taylor Swift.

stories.title: Ferry carrying 133 passengers and crew sinks off Guyana coast
stories.summary: Fifteen children are among 67 people rescued, officials say, with dozens still missing.

stories.title: World Cup final: Spain vs. Argentina — live updates

stories.summary: As the bombing starts again, it’s clear the president has dragged the US into a limitless fiasco – and the world into an economic quagmire Feckless and clueless, Donald Trump is lost in Iran, unable to find a way out of the disastrous war he started. Once again, the US military is pummelling the country and, increasingly, its civilian infrastructure. As before, this unlawful bludgeoning strengthens the resistance of a hardline regime that cares little for its people’s suffering. How often have T

stories.title: Dismissal of Ukraine’s defence minister highlights wider issues for Zelenskyy
stories.summary: Mykhailo Fedorov, celebrated by many for innovative, tech-driven approach, was sidelined for military old guard Volodomyr Zelenskyy’s abrupt dismissal of Ukraine’s youthful and innovative defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, at precisely the moment Kyiv appeared to be gaining advantages in several spheres of its war with Russia has exposed, not for the first time, a troubling flaw in the president’s leadership. The move, which has startled senior European officials and caused consternation, and d

stories.title: Ukraine drones target Russian warehouses and oil sites
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones have struck warehouses, an oil depot and other targets across Russia, killing nine people and injuring over 80 more. Kyiv said its strikes targeted suppliers for Moscow's ongoing invasion of Ukraine.

stories.title: US strikes Iran after 2 service members killed in Jordan
stories.summary: The US military struck Iran after two US soldiers were reported killed in an Iranian attack on a US base in Jordan. Meanwhile, Kuwait and Bahrain reported fresh Iranian attacks. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Flash flood warnings strike north-east US amid wildfire smoke from Canada
stories.summary: Poor air quality from wildfires continues to plague New York City as several neighborhoods hit by thunderstorms Saturday is delivering a double dose of dangerous weather across the north-eastern US, with smoky skies from Canadian wildfires giving way to severe thunderstorms that have already triggered a flash flood warning as torrential rain pounds parts of the region. After a brief break on Friday, smoke from the wildfires moved back into New York City and surrounding communities on Saturday, s

stories.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
stories.summary: Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials. According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora. Continue reading...

stories.title: Lashes, langers, bozzers and belly bachelors: a new book decodes Cork’s local slang
stories.summary: Sex, drink and religion are common motifs in the ‘extraordinarily rich’ colloquial vernacular of Ireland’s second city If Des MacHale had to nominate a favourite from the lexicon of insulting and inexplicable terms that comprise Cork slang, it would have to be “langer”. Depending on tone and context it can mean idiot, drunkenness or penis, a versatility that baffles outsiders and further enhances the word’s value. “Langer is an absolutely beautiful word. I’m very fond of it,” says MacHale. Conti

stories.title: Norway: Huge fire destroys 100 homes, sparks evacuations
stories.summary: Firefighters from other areas of Norway were drafted in to help tackle the blaze in the southern city of Drammen.

stories.title: Germany: CDU's Jens Spahn resigns amid surrogacy controversy
stories.summary: Surrogacy is not permitted in Germany and the conservative Christian Democratic Union (CDU) opposes its legalization. Spahn and his husband had their child through a surrogate mother in the US.

stories.title: Cuban dissident artist arrives in US after 5 years in prison
stories.summary: Luis Manuel Otero Alcantara, a prominent Cuban artist and dissident, was detained in July 2021 as he attempted to join unprecedented mass protests across the communist island.

stories.title: France's Mbappe sets World Cup scoring record with 22 goals
stories.summary: Kylian Mbappe has moved past Lionel Messi on the all-time World Cup goal-scoring record in a spectacular match for third place. England won the game 6-4.

stories.title: Andrew Tate, brother arrested in Miami, UK seeks extradition
stories.summary: Andrew and Tristan Tate were taken into custody by the US Marshals Service in Miami on a sealed warrant. The UK has requested their extradition on charges that include trafficking and rape.

stories.title: Russia's largest ballistic assault of war rocks Kyiv
stories.summary: Fires broke out across the Ukrainian capital after Russia's latest missile attack, which used the highest number of ballistic missiles of the war so far. Several people have been injured in Kyiv.

stories.title: Private jets flock to Montana - but locals can't afford the trailer park
stories.summary: Rents in Bozeman, Montana have skyrocketed as wealthy out-of-towners flock to the city to experience the American West.

stories.title: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
stories.summary: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.

stories.title: Jó hírek az égboltról: a következő napokban nem fogunk megsülni
stories.summary: A korábbi kánikulánál enyhébb időjárás várható, néhol zápor-zivatar is kialakulhat.

stories.title: „Szégyenletes” – Rabiot kiosztotta a társait az angolok ellen 6-4-re elvesztett bronzmeccs után
stories.summary: A francia középpályás szerint elfogadhatatlan volt az, ahogy a bronzmérkőzés első félidejében játszottak.

stories.title: Az Országos Diáktanáccsal egyeztetett Lannert Judit minisztériuma

A botrány után Athénba utazik, hogy egy gazdag család gyermekét tanítsa, és a bolhapiacon megpillant egy nőt, aki nyugtalanítóan ismerősnek tűnik számára. Ettől a pillanattól kezdve bárhol is jár Európában, a titokzatos hasonmás mindenütt felbukkan. Elsát gyerekkorában Arthur, a legendás zongoratanár fogadta örökbe,

stories.title: „Nem találom a szavakat, hogy elmondjam, milyen büszke vagyok rájuk. Megszakadt szívvel játszanak”

stories.title: MI a pályán: a Lenovo segített a 2026-os foci-vb legkritikusabb pillanatainál

Ahogy a 2026-os labdarúgó-világbajnokság elérkezett a mindent eldöntő fináléhoz, nemcsak a pályára lépő sztárok, hanem az eseményt a háttérből kiszolgáló technológiai gigasztráda is vizsgát tett. A torna hivatalos partnereként a Lenovo infrastruktúrája szinte valós időben dolgozta fel a futballtörténelem legnagyobb adatme

stories.title: Valaki megint felmászott egy vasúti kocsi tetejére, a MÁV alkalmazottai mentették meg az áramütéstől
stories.summary: Az eset Rákosszentmihályon történt, az ismeretlent később elfogták a rendőrök.

stories.title: Megható pillanat: az angolok érmet adtak a kapusnak, aki még a vb-keretben sem volt benne
stories.summary: Jason Steele az egész vb-n ott volt az angol válogatottal, az edzésekbe is besegített még úgy is, hogy tudta, egy percet sem játszhat a világbajnokságon.

stories.title: Infantino 15 milliárd dolláros vb-bevétellel betonozhatja be a FIFA-elnöki pozícióját
stories.summary: A Balogun-balhé után több európai futballszövetség elégedetlenkedett Infantinóval, de a pénz nagy úr.

stories.title: Egy test egy lélek ellen – így nézd a vb-döntőt
stories.summary: A 37 mérkőzés óta veretlen Spanyolország áll a futball örökös csúcsára igyekvő Lionel Messi és az argentinok útjában. Hatalmas taktikai csata várható, segítünk eligazodni, kit és mit érdemes figyelni a döntőben.

stories.title: Gajdos László szerint megszűnt Polt Péter védettsége
stories.summary: Gajdos azt írta, mostantól Poltnak is számot kell adnia a tetteiről a Vagyonvisszaszerzési Hivatal előtt.

stories.title: Nem népszerűek az ivószünetek, a FIFA felülvizsgálja őket
stories.summary: Mondja a FIFA technikai bizottságát vezető Arsène Wenger.

stories.title: Veszel egy használt autót, a nevedre írják, majd kiderül, hogy egy másik országban körözi a rendőrség
stories.summary: Az eredetiségvizsgálat alkalmatlan a kintről hozott autók előéletének kiderítésére, a fizetős lekérdező szolgáltatások szintén, és ezt tudják a bűnözők is. Csók Zsolt alezredessel, a Nemzetközi Bűnügyi Együttműködési Központ szakértőjével beszélgettünk.

stories.title: Átkutattak egy mezőt, amit a gyilkos jelölt meg. A keresés kezdete után 10 órával csontokat találtak
stories.summary: Egy már 30 év börtönre ítélt francia férfi most öt és fél év után bevallotta, hogy tényleg ő ölte meg a feleségét, és a holttest feltételezett helyét is felfedte. De minderre nem a megbánás vehette rá.

stories.title: Többen is megsérültek, elverte a jégeső Bad Bunny milánói koncertjét
stories.summary: Diónál is nagyobb jégdarabok potyogtak az égből, a telt házas koncert 80 ezer nézője közül többeket is eltalálva.

stories.title: A Politico szerint saját magát rúgta ki Sulyok Tamás
stories.summary: Sulyok Tamás szombaton kora este jelentette be, hogy aláírja az Alaptörvény 17. módosítását, amely megszüntette az ő köztársasági elnöki megbízatását is.

stories.title: Az űripar lehet a tőzsde következő nagy sztorija
stories.summary: Egyre inkább a befektetők terepe az ágazat, amely rendkívül tőkeigényes, miközben egyetlen technológiai kudarc vagy sikertelen rakétaindítás is komoly veszteségeket okozhat.

stories.title: Azt hittem, életem koncertje lesz, végül vaksötétben botorkáltunk a sárban a kijárat felé
stories.summary: A Telex újságírója is ott volt a jégeső miatt félbeszakadt milánói Bad Bunny koncerten. Kollégánk elmesélte, hogyan reagált a közönség a viharra, és hogyan jutottak ki a helyszínről.

stories.title: Trump helikopterrel futott be, indul a vb-döntő: Spanyolország–Franciaország 0-0
stories.summary: IShowSpeed és Post Malone minikoncertet tolt a vb-döntő előtt, a kezdés elé ígért Robbie Williams és Tom Cruise még várat magára. A döntő félidejében Madonna, Shakira és Justin Bieber is fellép. Spanyolország 2010 után nyerhet újra világbajnokságot, Argentína a harmadik címvédő ország lenne.

stories.title: Keresi a rendőrség a cserbenhagyó autóst, aki halálra gázolt egy biciklist
stories.summary: A kerékpáros zöld lámpánál ment volna át kerékpárúton Győr belvárosában.

stories.title: Európa-bajnok az U20-as férfi kézilabda válogatott
stories.summary: Hosszabbítás után győzték le 34-32-re Svédországot.

stories.title: Australia updates travel advice for Laos as charges announced over mass methanol poisoning
stories.summary: Distillery owner could face only three months in jail over alleged poisoning of Australian teenagers Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian government has increased its travel alert level for Laos, advising travellers to “exercise a high degree of caution” due to the risk of methanol poisoning. It comes as a distillery owner in Laos was charged after a mass methanol poisoning incident killed six foreign nationals, including the Australian backpackers Bianca J

stories.title: US launches seventh night of Iran strikes as Hormuz conflict escalates
stories.summary: Central Command says attacks were designed to ‘continue degrading Iranian military capabilities’ The US military said it had launched a seventh consecutive night of strikes on Iran on Friday night as fighting escalated over the strait of Hormuz. US Central Command, in a post on X, said the strikes, which began at 7pm GMT, were designed to “continue degrading Iranian military capabilities”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Why is Trump risking midterm disaster by resuming the unpopular Iran war?
stories.summary: Less than a month after hailing a ceasefire, Trump resumed strikes on Iran, a move experts warn could prolong the war and hurt Republicans before the midterms For half a century, Donald Trump has performed a public high-wire act based on high-stakes risks and shattering time-honored norms to get what he wants. The approach has paid off handsomely, helping him survive multiple bankruptcies to reach billionaire status and numerous legal and political scandals to be elected US president twice. Cont

stories.title: Cuba edges toward breakdown as power cuts and US meddling push society to brink
stories.summary: As Cuba swelters under six-month oil blockade imposed by US, tempers are fraying and unrest is growing When Cuba’s national grid collapses, as it did for the third time in 10 days on Tuesday, a collective groan spreads across its cities and people wonder, again, whether the island’s antiquated electricity system may soon become unrecoverable. The 777-mile long Caribbean island of 9.5 million people has been sweltering under a six-month-long oil blockade imposed by the US, part of a pressure camp

stories.title: US forces strike Iran for seventh consecutive night
stories.summary: Iran's Fars state news agency reported that Iranian forces had responded by attacking US allies and bases in the region, including the Ali Al Salem airbase in Kuwait.

stories.title: ICE arrests human rights lawyer who fled Chinese crackdown
stories.summary: Arrest in Pennsylvania of Wu Shaoping, who is awaiting asylum decision, raises fears of deportation and persecution A Chinese human rights lawyer has been arrested by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), raising concerns he could be deported to China where he would face persecution. Wu Shaoping fled China at the end of 2019 amid a crackdown on human rights lawyers. He travelled to the US on a tourist visa and made an asylum claim in 2020, for which he is still awaiting a decision. Conti

stories.title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
stories.summary: Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters. The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in

stories.title: The hill I will die on: Parisian waiters are not rude – they’re just badly misunderstood | Helen Massy-Beresford
stories.summary: After living here for years, I can see through that old stereotype. My tip: if your server is not full of bonhomie, why not try saying ‘bonjour’? Parisian waiters are professionals, providing an excellent service – they are not rude or unfriendly, just sometimes slightly misunderstood. No, really, hear me out. We’re all familiar with the trope of the rude Parisian waiter, looking down their nose at your inferior wine choice. They have been called “brusque and unwelcoming”, “snooty and rude” by t

stories.title: Air quality warnings remain in place across US as wildfire smoke continues to swathe country
stories.summary: Rain could alleviate conditions in mid-Atlantic and north-east, with World Cup final expected to go ahead on Sunday Warnings of dangerous conditions are expected to remain in place on Saturday across swathes of the US, amid uncertainty about where the heavy wildfire smoke swirling from the Canadian province of Ontario and the US state of Minnesota will head next. Some parts of the US mid-Atlantic and north-east regions will continue to endure poor air quality until Saturday afternoon, where ther

stories.title: Germany’s CDU parliamentary leader resigns after using surrogacy to become parent
stories.summary: Jens Spahn had criticised ‘rented wombs’ and his party is strongly opposed to surrogacy, which is banned in Germany A senior German politician and ally of the chancellor, Friedrich Merz, has resigned as parliamentary group leader of the Christian Democrat (CDU) party after he and his husband used a surrogate mother to become parents, a practice he has criticised in the past and his party is vehemently opposed to. Surrogacy is banned in Germany, a policy Jens Spahn refused to relax when he was he

stories.title: ‘It’s only going to get worse’: wildfires forcing firefighters to make impossible choices
stories.summary: As the climate crisis fuels more intense blazes, pushing them to new parts of the world, those tackling them are forced to ration resources and decide which to fight César Alcaraz had only just become a firefighter in the late 1990s when he found himself ambushed by a fast-moving blaze. Barely able to breathe and with no more water left in his truck, he and his colleagues fled an inferno ravaging Spain’s Montgó mountain region, wishing their bosses had sent more support. But nearly three decades

stories.title: Franciaország–Anglia 4-6
Két amerikai katona meghalt, egy pedig eltűnt a Jordániát ért iráni légitámadásban
stories.summary: Irán és az Egyesült Államok ismét egymást lövi, miután múlt héten összeomlott a felek közötti tűzszüneti megállapodás.

stories.title: Anglia hatvan év után nyert világbajnoki érmet, tízgólos meccsen verték a franciákat
stories.summary: Tízgólos, őrült fordulatokat hozó meccsen Anglia szerezte meg a bronzérmet a 2026-os futball-világbajnokságon. Az első félidő után már 4-0-ra vezettek az angolok, de a szünet után a franciák felzárkóztak, és az egyenlítéshez is nagyon közel jártak. Végül az angol válogatott 6-4-re nyert és 60 év után szereztek ismét világbajnoki érmet. Kylian Mbappé két gólt lőtt a meccsen, amivel vb-rekordot döntött.

stories.title: Tarr Béla születésnapja alkalmából a Sátántangót vetíti a televízió
stories.summary: A Nobel-díjas Krasznahorkai László regényéből készült hét és fél órás filmet reklámszünetek nélkül nézhetjük végig.

stories.title: Az újságíró, aki őrültnek tettette magát, hogy bemutassa egy elmegyógyintézet borzalmait
stories.summary: A 18-19. században nagy divatja volt az elmegyógyintézet-turizmusnak, ami jó bevételi forrás volt az intézeteknek, és a PR-nak sem ártott. A bemutatók színfalai mögött azonban gyakori volt a kegyetlenség és a kínzás, amit Nellie Bly beépülve mutatott be.

stories.title: Szabadtéri koncerthelyszínként éledhet újjá Pécs egyik lepusztult strandja, ha a környékbeliek hagyják
stories.summary: Lakossági tiltakozás indult Pécsen az egykori Balokány-fürdő fejlesztése ellen. A zajterhelés miatt aggódóknak a polgármester is azzal érvelt, hogy szükség lenne a városban egy koncerthelyszínre.

stories.title: Újabb csapásokat mért Iránra az Egyesült Államok, miután két amerikai katona meghalt egy iráni támadásban
stories.summary: Irán pedig Kuvaitban hajtott végre dróncsapásokat az iráni állami média szerint.

stories.title: Oroszország a háború egyik legnagyobb rakétatámadását zúdította Kijevre
stories.summary: A támadások során egy ember meghalt és tizenheten megsebesültek, lakó- és irodaépületek is megrongálódtak, egy metróállomás mennyezete pedig leomlott.

stories.title: Őrizetbe vették a Tate fivéreket az Egyesült Államokban újabb brit vádemelések után
stories.summary: Ügyvédjük politikai indíttatású támadásnak nevezte a vádpontokat.

stories.title: Germany's 5,000 heat deaths: What the numbers reveal
stories.summary: More than 5,100 people in Germany died from heat-related causes this year until the end of June, the Robert Koch Institute reports. But how can researchers prove that someone died because of soaring temperatures?

stories.title: Germany cracks down on money laundering, tax fraud
stories.summary: The German government plans to crack down on tax fraud, money laundering and illegally acquired assets with more audits and stiffer penalties.

stories.title: Why did Jens Spahn's surrogacy spark debate in Germany?
stories.summary: Jens Spahn, a key ally of Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is stepping down amid a controversy surrounding his decision to have a child via a surrogate mother. He had opposed legalizing surrogacy in Germany.

stories.title: Reproductive medicine laws in Germany 'shockingly unjust'
stories.summary: Infertility affects one in six women in Germany. However, access to reproductive medicine is not available to all. Surrogacy as well as egg donation are banned.

stories.title: Segregation in Israel: Separate streets for men and women?
stories.summary: An Israeli city with a large ultra-Orthodox Jewish population wants to segregate men and women on certain streets. Israeli politicians have voted to allow university courses to do the same.

stories.title: One anti-war critic fined, another held as Russia clamps down on dissent
stories.summary: While Boris Nadezhdin is barred from running for parliament, blogger Ilya Remeslo is remanded in custody.

stories.title: White House defends Argentina team over Falklands banner
stories.summary: The comments could further fuel the row over the incident, which has seen Downing Street back calls for Fifa to investigate.

stories.title: Kim Jong Un was meant to be their only idol - then North Koreans discovered K-pop
stories.summary: Defectors tell the BBC that despite restrictions, K-pop has cut through in the stifling dictatorship.

stories.title: How these twins' rare growth disorder could provide the key to preventing cancer
stories.summary: Researchers have found that people with Laron syndrome have a lower incidence of cancer than the wider population.

stories.title: What this desert city can teach the world about tackling heat deaths
stories.summary: Maricopa County in Arizona has some of the hottest temperatures, but it has reduced the number of heat-related deaths.

stories.title: He made history in Congress - then the Supreme Court changed its mind
stories.summary: Residents say they're afraid that if Shomari Figures loses his seat, their federal-funding could disappear with him.

stories.title: Brad Pitt's children are dropping their dad's surname - here's why I did the same
stories.summary: Hannah had used her mother's maiden name on her social media for years before she legally made the change.

stories.title: Control, threats, disfiguring surgery: My life inside Jeffrey Epstein's 'cult'
stories.summary: "Anya" gives the BBC a rare account of how sex-criminal financier Epstein lured and abused his “assistants”.

stories.title: Indian activist on hunger strike for 20 days forcibly taken to hospital
stories.summary: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has now begun an indefinite fast in Sonam Wangchuk's place.

stories.title: Space start-up launches India’s first commercial rocket designed for orbit
stories.summary: India's first space-tech unicorn Skyroot Aerospace successfully launched Vikram-1 on Saturday.

stories.title: Helicopters dump water as huge Norway fire destroys homes
stories.summary: The blaze has destroyed more than 100 homes in Drammen and has spread to a nearby forest, authorities say.

stories.title: Laos says it can't determine cause of tourist deaths linked to methanol
stories.summary: Officials say they have no evidence of poisoning over the 2024 deaths because autopsies were not conducted.

stories.title: German politician resigns over surrogacy child controversy
stories.summary: Jens Spahn faced criticism over his use of a surrogate, a ban on which he previously backed.

stories.title: Deaths from Venezuela earthquakes rise above 5,000
stories.summary: Venezuela has been able to unlock €302 million of funding from the International Monetary Fund to support recovery and reconstruction following the devastating earthquakes that hit the northern coastal region last month.

stories.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
stories.summary: Canadian leader Mark Carney says both the US and Canada have an equal responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say are worsening wildfire conditions.

stories.title: US launches new wave of strikes on Iran as Israel vows 'powerful' response to Tehran
stories.summary: Iran says the latest US strikes killed at least 50 people and wounded 517. Washington said the attacks were retaliation for a strike on a US base in Jordan that killed two US service members, left one missing and injured four others.

stories.title: Trump teleprompter operator suspended for alleged prediction market bets on speeches
stories.summary: US media had reported that Gabriel Perez, who has been operating Trump’s teleprompter since 2016, used his inside knowledge to win more than $100,000 (€85,900) betting on what the president would say in big speeches.

stories.title: Trump revives claims of election interference during address to nation
stories.summary: The US president said he would declassify intelligence data that revealed China had illicitly acquired 220 million US voter files. Trump's claim contradicts reviews that have found no evidence of significant interference in the 2020 election.

stories.title: Watch: US Navy Blue Angels' low flyover leaves beachgoers stunned
stories.summary: US Navy Blue Angels jets flew low over a crowded Florida beach, startling beachgoers as chairs and tents were blown away and children covered their ears. The dramatic flyover sparked renewed debate over the safety of low-altitude military demonstrations.

stories.title: Euronews aboard EU warships in the Red Sea: Europe eyes wider mandate as Houthi threat grows
stories.summary: In a special report from EU patrol ships in Djibouti, Kaja Kallas told Euronews that the 27-member bloc was discussing expanding Aspides' mandate and that ships pledged to a Hormuz coalition could be redeployed to the Red Sea in the interim.

stories.title: China ‘strongly dissatisfied’ with nationalisation of British Steel
stories.summary: Move dealt ‘severe blow to Chinese companies’ confidence in investing in the UK’, says Ministry of Commerce China’s government has said it is “strongly dissatisfied” with the decision to nationalise British Steel this week, 15 months after the UK government intervened to prevent the closure of its steelworks in Scunthorpe and the loss of 4,000 jobs. On Thursday, British Steel was brought under public ownership to protect “the future of steel production”, the government announced. Continue readin

stories.title: Biodiversity fears as human-bred hybrid fish integrate into Philippines lake
stories.summary: Escaped flowerhorn cichlids are causing concern for native species and about parasites capable of infecting humans Escaped ornamental aquarium fish have integrated into a local ecosystem in the Philippines, but scientists say they may be threatening the native biodiversity of the lake. Flowerhorn cichlids – human-bred hybrid fish prized for their bright-gold colour and prominent head humps – are believed to have escaped from breeding facilities into Lake Sampaloc, which sits in a volcanic crater

stories.title: German tenants need better heat protection
stories.summary: Heat waves are becoming longer, hotter and more frequent. Record temperatures have left many tenants struggling. Now Chancellor Friedrich Merz has said the building law needs to be amended.

stories.title: Iran's oil supply threat extends beyond Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Oil and gas producers in the Gulf are seeking alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz as traffic again comes under fire. But is Iran also turning its attention to the pipelines meant to bypass the waterway?

stories.title: How AI and satellites help fight wildfires
stories.summary: Wildfires are raging across Europe, Canada and beyond. To successfully fight these fires, speed is essential. A German startup uses satellite data and AI to spot fires early.

stories.title: The EU climate policy the world copied is under fire — what's at stake?
stories.summary: The EU Commission has proposed to weaken Europe's emissions trading system, a global blueprint for pricing carbon and cutting CO2. How effective is the scheme and where does it fall short?

stories.title: How new PM Andy Burnham will change UK's international role
stories.summary: From northwest England to the global stage: Andy Burnham becomes Britain's new prime minister on Monday. How will the former mayor of Manchester tackle international affairs?

stories.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's next prime minister
stories.summary: Andy Burnham has been confirmed as the successor to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. The former Manchester mayor has become a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

stories.title: Cities are getting smarter about water and other eco wins
stories.summary: From flood-resistant infrastructure to new ways of securing drinking water, cities are adapting to the growing climate pressures of a hotter, drier world. Plus, tourists pitching in to help sea turtles.

stories.title: Merz and Macron: A Franco-German week of harmony
stories.summary: Friedrich Merz and Emmanuel Macron did everything they could this week to demonstrate a close partnership. This is likely because both fear that the next French president could be nationalist Marine Le Pen.

stories.title: Spain battles major Aragon wildfire amid heatwave
stories.summary: Spain has deployed more than 400 firefighters and 19 aircraft to combat a fast-moving wildfire in the northeastern region of Aragon after flames swept across around 7,600 hectares by 16 July.

stories.title: Rubik’s Cube stars shine at European speedcubing event in the Netherlands
stories.summary: More than 1,000 speedcubers from 53 countries are competing in Arnhem, the Netherlands, from 16 to 19 July 2026. Events range from blindfolded solves to solving while wearing oven gloves.

stories.title: US airstrikes hit Iranian civilian sites as Tehran attacks Gulf neighbours
stories.summary: In a primetime address to the nation, Trump said the US was "winning big in Iran" and the public would see "the fruits of that labour". However, a deal seems far out of reach as fighting in the region continues to escalate.

stories.title: Japan revises imperial succession law but retains ban on female emperors
stories.summary: Nationwide opinion polls in Japan have suggested broad public support for a female emperor, but some conservative political figures remain opposed to the idea.

stories.title: Qatar’s air defence intercepts fresh Iranian missile barrage over Doha
stories.summary: Iran has unleashed a new wave of attacks across the Gulf, targeting Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan, following Tehran’s continuous threats of dragging the Gulf countries back into its escalating confrontation with the US over the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Chile assesses damage after deadly storm surge floods coastal city of Penco
stories.summary: Chile's central coast has been hit by an exceptional storm surge that flooded homes and damaged infrastructure. Authorities are urging residents to stay away from the shoreline as dangerous sea conditions continue.

stories.title: Kim Jong Un hosts top Chinese official in Pyongyang
stories.summary: China has sought to reassert its position in the Korean peninsula in recent years. Chinese President Xi Jinping made his own visit to Pyongyang in June as part of this push.

stories.title: Bodycam footage shows airlift of girl stranded by Texas floods
stories.summary: Footage shows a rescuer reaching a family of three stranded by floods that destroyed their house in Uvalde county.

stories.title: CIA declassifies evidence of alleged Venezuelan election fraud in 2012
stories.summary: CIA documents declassified by Donald Trump confirm that the Venezuelan regime had, from 2012, a system capable of altering up to 1.5 million votes, though they do not prove fraud in every election mentioned.

stories.title: China rescuers race to find trapped people after landslide
stories.summary: China launched a large-scale rescue operation after a landslide struck Pengshui County in Chongqing municipality on 17 July, burying part of a residential area and trapping an unknown number of people.

stories.title: Xi hails 'new level' in ties as China and Kazakhstan sign €11.4bn in deals
stories.summary: Xi Jinping and Kassym-Jomart Tokayev signed more than 70 commercial agreements on the sidelines of the World AI Conference in Shanghai, including Central Asia's first battery plant with CATL and a new Caspian port terminal.

stories.title: China rejects Trump's election interference accusations as 'groundless'
stories.summary: At a daily briefing in Beijing, a spokesperson called on the US to stop making what it described as 'groundless accusations' against China.

stories.title: Timelapse shows World Cup final stadium nearly vanish in smoky haze
stories.summary: MetLife stadium in New Jersey, where the World Cup final will be held, was shrouded in haze caused by hundreds of wildfires in Canada.

stories.title: World Cup organisers 'monitoring' wildfire smoke ahead of New Jersey final
stories.summary: Officials in many cities urged residents to stay indoors or wear masks outside as air quality reached unhealthy to hazardous levels, meaning it posed a health risk to everyone,

stories.title: 7.3 magnitude earthquake strikes Mexico and Guatemala coasts, tsunami alert lifted
stories.summary: A 7.3 magnitude earthquake shook the coasts of Mexico and Guatemala on Friday, its epicentre off Chiapas at about 15 km deep.

stories.title: EU foreign policy chief Kallas visits EU naval mission in the Red Sea
stories.summary: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas visited personnel serving with the EU's ASPIDES and ATALANTA naval missions, receiving operational briefings and meeting troops deployed to help protect shipping routes in the Red Sea. Euronews correspondent Toby Gregory accompanied Kallas during the visit.

stories.title: Ukraine lawmakers approve Koretskyi as new PM amid protests
stories.summary: It is President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's second Cabinet shakeup in a year. He faces criticism for dismissing reformist Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

stories.title: US slaps 25% tariffs on Brazil with election looming
stories.summary: The US will impose a 25% tariff on most imports from Brazil starting later this month. It's the first action in the Trump administration's new tariff strategy, after the Supreme Court struck down its prior impositions.

stories.title: Germany massively expands federal police powers
stories.summary: The German government has increased the powers of its federal police, especially in the use of AI, surveillance and drone defenses. But experts warn of constitutional challenges.

stories.title: Kyiv hit again as Ukraine reports two dead in Russian strike
stories.summary: Russian missiles killed two people and wounded five, including a child, in Kyiv, Ukraine, on 16 July 2026.

stories.title: Kyiv protesters rally after Zelenskyy sacks defence minister Fedorov
stories.summary: Hundreds protest in Kyiv after President Zelenskyy dismisses Defence Minister Fedorov, a moderniser credited with slowing Russia’s advance.

stories.title: France holds annual Bastille Day parade in Paris – video
stories.summary: France marked its annual Bastille Day celebration in Paris with a parade of military regiments, including foreign troops. Leaders from the 'coalition of the willing' were invited to watch the parade Continue reading...

stories.title: Spain and Gibraltar celebrate as border fence falls after signing of ‘historic deal’
stories.summary: Treaty came into effect at midnight, eliminating border controls on land frontier with British overseas territory Europe live – latest updates UK politics live – latest updates Spain and Gibraltar are celebrating the fall of the last frontier fence in western Europe after the signing of a post-Brexit deal that brings an end to border checks for residents, tourists and the thousands of Spanish workers who cross into the British overseas territory every day. The agreement, which was signed in Brus

stories.title: More than 500 feared dead after reports of two shipwrecks off Myanmar, UN says
stories.summary: Vessels believed to have departed from Myanmar in late June, with mostly Muslim Rohingya minority onboard The United Nations has said more than 500 people are feared dead after reports of two large shipwrecks off Myanmar since late June. The UN’s International Organization for Migration (IOM) and its refugee agency UNHCR voiced alarm in a joint statement at reports “that two boats carrying more than 500 people may have capsized off the coast of Myanmar in recent days”. Continue reading...

stories.title: How the 2016 coup attempt changed Turkey for good
stories.summary: Ten years ago, parts of the Turkish military attempted to overthrow the government of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. The consequences of the failed coup continue to shape Turkey to this day. DW looks back.

stories.title: No support for Jews fleeing Nazi Germany at Evian conference
stories.summary: In July 1938, a conference was held in Evian, France, to address the issue of Jews being persecuted by the Nazis in Germany and Austria. Almost nobody in the world was willing to take them in as refugees.

stories.title: East-West border death zone now Germany's biodiversity oasis
stories.summary: Many endangered species now live in the area that once formed the border between East and West Germany. Environment Minister Carsten Schneider recently visited the "Green Belt," and DW went along for the ride.

stories.title: AI: Why Europe is falling behind, and how it can catch up
stories.summary: Europe trails the US and China in artificial intelligence. Economist and Nobel laureate Philippe Aghion says better research funding, venture capital and AI innovation can help it catch up.

stories.title: Monaco assassination plot takes a new turn
stories.summary: A woman suspected of plotting to assassinate a Ukrainian-Cypriot businessman in Monaco has been found dead in Ukraine. Two men with ties to Ukraine's intelligence services have now been brought into custody.

stories.title: Tehran vows to 'crush' regional infrastructure if US hits Iranian sites
stories.summary: Iran said its response to any US strike on its infrastructure would result in destruction across the region, as multiple reports said Tehran had asked the Houthis to be ready to close the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, threatening a second global shipping chokepoint.

stories.title: Supporters welcome France's assisted dying law
stories.summary: Supporters welcomed France's assisted dying law after parliament approved the landmark legislation, calling it "a huge relief" for people with incurable illnesses. The law still requires approval from France's Constitutional Council.

stories.title: Watch: What to know as Genoa bridge disaster trial ends
stories.summary: More than 50 defendants have been on trial in Italy over the 2018 bridge collapse which killed 43 people.

stories.title: Woman speaks out on husband being pulled from Ryanair plane – video
stories.summary: Svetlana Grković and her husband, Ljubisa Karović, had just settled into a Ryanair flight last week when a loud bang pierced the hum of engines. Within seconds, she saw her 61-year-old husband being sucked out of the plane through a dislodged cabin window. Karovic is now being treated in hospital in Thessaloniki with severe neck and arm injuries, Maksimovic said ‘If we die, we die together’: wife of Ryanair passenger almost sucked through window speaks Continue reading...

stories.title: Uganda calls for travel restrictions to be lifted after last Ebola patient discharged
stories.summary: Country begins 42-day countdown to outbreak being declared officially over, as numbers continue to rise in neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo Uganda has started lobbying countries to lift Ebola-related travel restrictions after discharging its last confirmed Ebola patient from hospital. The discharge of a Congolese national from the Mulago national referral hospital’s isolation centre in Kampala on Thursday triggered the start of a 42-day countdown required by the World Health Organiz

stories.title: UK aid cuts ‘reduce bilateral support to some African countries by 90%’
stories.summary: Critics say Foreign Office figures send ‘global message about the role the country wants to play on international stage’ Labour’s foreign aid cuts mean reductions of as much as 90% in the bilateral support the UK will give to some African countries, Foreign Office figures show. The department’s annual report includes a long-awaited breakdown of how the reduction in the aid budget will affect individual countries for the next three years. Continue reading...

stories.title: T. rex 'Gus' sells for record-breaking $50.1 million
stories.summary: The Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton surpassed its pre-sale estimate and set a new auction record for a dinosaur fossil.

stories.title: Germany's Merz receives death threat from Iranian newspaper
stories.summary: In retaliation for Khamenei's death, a Tehran daily listed 13 world leaders, including German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, as targets. Politicians voiced concern.
